LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND FOR SANCTIONS - MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST DEFENDANT ON OCTOBER 15, 2012 IS VACATED AND SET ASIDE PURSUANT TO CCP 473(D) BECAUSE PERSONAL SERVICE ON OFFICE RECEPTIONIST WAS NOT PROPER UNDER CCP 416.10. EVEN IF PERSONAL SERVICE WERE OTHERWISE PROPER, THE COURT WOULD VACATE AND SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT UNDER CCP 473(B) ON ACCOUNT OF MISTAKE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE INTENTIONAL TORTS (CAUSES OF ACTION 1-3). PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND IN GOOD FAITH FACTS TO SUPPORT A RESPONDEAT SUPERIOR THEORY OF LIABILITY WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 4 FOR NEGLIGENCE. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS ARE SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A NEGLIGENT HIRING, TRAINING, SUPERVISING, RETENTION, ETC. THEORY OF LIABILITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Off Calendar Mar-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: NIKOLAUS W. REED, ESQ., 415.940.7766, FOR PLAINTIFF IRENE GIAMPAOLI.
DANIEL C. ZAMORA, ESQ., 415.772.9639, FOR DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC.

RULING: MATTER ARGUED. AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Continued To May-08-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Continued To Mar-27-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nikolaus Reed, Esq., 415.940.7766, for plaintiff Irene Giampaoli.
Daniel Zamora, Esq., 415.433.1400, for defendant Tenderloin Housing Clinic.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend as to the intentional torts (causes of action 1-3). Plaintiff has leave to amend in good faith facts to support a respondeat superior theory of liability WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. The demurrer is overruled as to cause of action 4 for negligence. Plaintiff's allegations are sufficient to support a negligent hiring, training, supervising, retention, etc. theory of liability. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2013 Continued To Feb-06-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to February 6, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. The opposing party is to provide a courtesy copy of the opposition in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Please attach a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The Court has the motion and reply. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Mtn To Vacate Default Ans For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INCORPORATED'S Motion To Vacate Default And For Sanctions - Motion to vacate default is Granted. The default entered against defendant on October 15, 2012 is vacated and set aside pursuant to CCP 473(d) because personal service on office receptionist was not proper under CCP 416.10. Even if personal service were otherwise proper, the Court would vacate and set aside the default judgment under CCP 473(b) on account of mistake or excusable neglect. Sanctions are denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/MJM
